revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東殿塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11433419|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}}}}
[[File:Tonotono-tsuka_stereo.jpg|thumb|350px|A stereogram aerial photo (1979) of {{ill|Higashi Tonozuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (top) and {{ill|Nishi Tonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Tonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom) {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}]]
'''Higashi Tonozuka Kofun''' (東殿塚古墳) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara Prefecture|1=Tenri, Nara}}.
== Overview == Based on the excavated {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, this tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. It is positioned directly east of the slightly older and larger {{ill|Nishi Tonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Tonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, which measures 234 meters in length. Both tumuli are located on a slope, oriented along a north-south axis, and situated at an altitude of approximately 140 meters. From the top of the mound, there is a panoramic view of the entire Nara Basin.
== Kofun Shape == The tumulus has a length of 139 meters. The rear circular mound (rear circle) is slightly elliptical along the main axis, while the front square mound (front part) is elongated. The ratio of the rear circle to the front part is approximately 1:1.5. The main axis runs north-south, creating an elevation difference on the east and west sides. The apex of the rear circle has been disturbed and is irregular in shape. The front part flattens near the constricted section and rises into a trapezoidal shape at the ends. Two rows of haniwa and stone arrangements were identified in this section. Cylindrical haniwa likely encircled the mound along its upper slopes.
== Excavated Items and Haniwa Ship Drawings == Archaeological investigations revealed a ceremonial platform-like structure in the southwestern section of the front part, which yielded numerous haniwa fragments. Among these was a restored elliptical cylindrical haniwa measuring 64 cm in height and 50 cm in maximum diameter. Notably, three haniwa pieces displayed ship drawings incised with a spatula, referred to as Ship Drawings No. 1, 2, and 3.
The most detailed, Ship Drawing No. 1, depicts a gondola-shaped vessel with seven oars, suggesting it was a large ship rowed by 14 individuals. The ship is shown with its sails filled with wind, interpreted as racing across the sea.
From the trapezoidal extension on the western side of the front part, other artifacts such as trumpet-shaped haniwa, cylindrical haniwa, elliptical cylindrical haniwa, and {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|qid=Q1377111|lt=earthenware|1=Earthenware}} were recovered. Some cylindrical haniwa featured fins and decorative cutouts in tomoe, square, rectangular, or circular shapes.
Among the earthenware, many intact and fragmented ritual vessels were identified. These included items from various regions such as the San'in, Omi, and Tokai regions, indicating the presence of interregional exchange. These vessels are considered among the earliest examples of their type.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Nishi Tonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Tonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
